name: Monstera obliqua 'Trinidad Locality Form'
aboutText: The Monstera obliqua 'Trinidad Locality Form' is a highly sought-after hemiepiphytic climber native to the tropical rainforests of Trinidad. This form is distinguished by its nearly entire, slightly broader ovate-elliptic leaves that exhibit sparse, limited perforation—a unique trait compared to more fenestrated variations of the species. It typically inhabits the dim, humid understory, rooting in deep leaf litter or thin humus. Its delicate, thin lamina and lightly bullate texture make it a prized specimen for dedicated aroid collectors.

fieldNotes.title: Caribbean Obliqua
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: A Trinidad locality form of Monstera obliqua from the Caribbean island, which is geologically and biogeographically continuous with the Venezuelan mainland rather than with the Antillean island chain. Trinidad's flora contains numerous South American species that reached the island via the former land connection to Venezuela. Rare, at accessible price tier. Trinidad obliqua — if genuinely obliqua rather than adansonii — represents an interesting biogeographic data point for understanding the species' distribution across its full range including island populations.
